title: The Lilin after Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: The Lilin after Instrumentality             Quote Post by Replicator » Tue Dec 11, 2012 9:06 pm Forgive me of this question sounds too strange even for EvaGeeks.org, what do you think the Lilin's tangible form have been once Instrumentality was completed? All individual humans have lost their own physical bodies, and absorbed into the Black Moon. Would the appearance of "Humanity as a God" just be the GNR holding Lilith's Egg, standing on the Earth? I'll even consider some alternative paths Instrumentality could have taken. If Gendo had had his way, and became the dominant soul of Lilith/Adam, he would just take over what Rei did in EoE. How would he have restored Yui, when he wasn't even tanged? I know this might be going too far, but I've always wanted to know how a post-Instrumentality "18th Angel" would be like. Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Tue Dec 11, 2012 10:28 pm Well, thinking about the ritual as the FAR intended it; merging all the souls of the planet into a Moon (Black Moon in this case) to be shot out off into space, so the whole damned Human Condition mess can play out again on another planet somewhere in another galaxy/solar system, far, far away. Gendo just wanted to become one, or merge, with Yui in the core of Unit-01, didn't he? So they could float through space together forever in eternal bliss? That, and the fact that he/they would be a "god", possessing both Fruits. Something like that anyway... He sure as hell didn't want what Seele wanted; "Death creates nothing!" Edit: Fixed quote.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 11, 2012 11:04 pm I doubt Gendo's plans involved only Yui, ep 25 makes it very clear that he has plans for all of humanity and genuinely thinks it's our only chance or survival. Again, his last conversation with SEELE again mentions his different plans for humanitiy's further evolution, he sounds almost a bit heroic there, "Death creates nothing!" As I got it, SEELE = Involves the LoL and the Evangelions, not So much Adam or lillith or at least not them fusing - they implied in ep 24 that they're through with anything Adam-based. Follow the "divine plan" in some way, tang everyone, further process human souls into something less recognizable than what Gendo's plan would produce. Gendo = Involves Lillith & Adam, Fuck the FAR's intentions, get more power than our creators ever intended, evolve to some higher form, leave a tad more of our "selves" intact that what SEELE has in mind, and spent eternity in some idyllic LCL puddle, or possibly EVA 01, with Yui and the rest of the family. At very least, Fuyu seems to consider Gendo the lesser of two evils. From the semi-philosophic talk they sprout, I'd more or less equate SEELE with your basic fundamentalist nutjobs, with Gendo being closer to a radical Transhumanist, less keen on destiny, more science-y, (ep 12!) but still pretty misantropic/pessimistic.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 12, 2012 2:53 pm I doubt Gendo's plans involved only Yui, ep 25 makes it very clear that he has plans for all of humanity and genuinely thinks it's our only chance or survival.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 12, 2012 4:21 pm It's quite possible that if you happened to be outsystem when shit went down, you'd not be able to tell the difference between a SEELE scenario cosmic egg staring into its own navel and a Gendo polis with a thriving noösphere.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Dec 13, 2012 6:23 am The 'ark' plan is what Gendo/Yui/Fuyutsuki were originally working towards, but it's not at all clear that they still agreed with that 14 years later. For all we know Gendo only meant for it to be him, Rei and Yui in there forever. The EoTV good upload thing may have been something Yui came up with after her perspective got shifted. We don't know exactly where Fuyu stands on any of them except that he's against Seele's vision. In EoE Rei seemed to want yet another variation.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Dec 13, 2012 7:20 am AuraTwilight wrote: As for the final product of SEELE's intended scenario, it'd basically just be the Black Moon floating around.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Dec 13, 2012 2:43 pm It's debatable how much that's in line with their plan as presented in the anime and how much is an alternate scenario they'd be equally satisfied with. Either way we have a "God" that doesn't DO anything, at all. It just floats around, content and possibly even unaware.
